generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only identifiers (features, properties) visible in the AADL model when writing AGREE contracts.
Ensure helper declarations (eq, const) have correct types (int, real, bool) matching their expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` helpers to define reusable expressions within AGREE contracts.
Range checks in AGREE typically use boolean expressions like `value >= lower_limit and value <= upper_limit`.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers (types, properties) into AGREE that are not present in the AADL model.
Avoid adding architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on fixing the contract itself.
Do not place guarantee statements within component implementation annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction details (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, owners)
AGREE contract generation and fusion process
